title: Cat Ate Moldy Food: Risks, Symptoms, and Triage Guide
meta_desc: If your cat ate moldy food, they may be at risk for mycotoxin poisoning. Learn about symptoms, urgency levels, and how to help your vet.
content_html:
TL;DR: Moldy food ingestion can cause life-threatening neurological issues in cats; seek immediate veterinary care if your cat shows signs of tremors, stumbling, or vomiting.

What is mycotoxin poisoning and how does it affect my cat?

When a cat consumes moldy food from the trash, they are often ingesting tremorgenic mycotoxins. These are toxic substances produced by fungi that can cause severe neurological and digestive issues in pets. Even a small amount of moldy bread, cheese, or compost can contain enough toxins to trigger a reaction.

Is it an emergency if my cat eats moldy food?

  • The urgency level for mold ingestion is Medium.
  • While some cats may only experience mild gastrointestinal upset, others can develop life-threatening tremors or seizures.
  • If your cat is showing signs of stumbling, shaking, or persistent vomiting, you should contact an emergency veterinarian immediately.
  • Early intervention is key to preventing the toxins from being fully absorbed into the bloodstream.

How can a photo of the moldy food help the veterinarian?

  • If possible, safely take a photo of the moldy item your cat consumed.
  • This visual information is incredibly helpful for veterinary staff and poison control experts in identifying the type of mold or food source.
  • A photo allows the medical team to better predict the severity of the reaction and determine the best course of treatment.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Ingestion of moldy food by cats can lead to mycotoxicosis, with the specific risks dependent on the type of mycotoxin present. Commonly affected food items include cereal grains, cheese, fruit, meats, nuts, and refrigerated foods. Clinical signs can vary, ranging from acute diarrhea, respiratory distress, and hemorrhagic gastroenteritis to immunosuppression and death. Chronic exposure may result in ulceration of the gastrointestinal tract and unthriftiness. Management includes immediate cessation of the suspect feed, thorough inspection and cleaning of storage and feeding equipment, and analysis of the feed for known mycotoxins.

Chapter: Toxicology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 3009)

faqs.question: What are the first signs of mold poisoning in cats?
faqs.answer: The most common early signs include vomiting, drooling, agitation, and a slight trembling or twitching of the muscles.

faqs.question: Should I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: No. You should never attempt to induce vomiting in a cat at home unless specifically instructed to do so by a licensed veterinarian, as this can lead to aspiration pneumonia.

faqs.question: How long does it take for symptoms to appear?
faqs.answer: Symptoms of mycotoxin poisoning usually appear within 30 minutes to a few hours after ingestion.
